g.text: Slab Structure: The Cache Line Connection
Category | Size Range | Alignment | Typical Usage
Tiny | 8 - 64 B | 8 - 16 B | Small structs, pointers
Small | 72 B - 4 KB| 16 - 64 B | String buffers, vectors
Large | 4 KB - 4 MB| 4 KB (Page)| SoA arrays, Large buffers
Huge | > 4 MB | 4 KB+ | mmap() direct, Large matrices

ol.li: Size Classes: jemalloc treats allocations of similar sizes identically, easing layout planning.
Slab Alignment: Slabs are page-aligned; slots are bin-aligned (e.g., 64-byte bins = cache alignment).
Spatial Locality: Contiguous arrays (like our SoA x[], y[]) fit neatly into slabs/pages.
Manual Control: Use aligned_alloc(64, ...) for SIMD (AVX/AVX-512) to ensure hardware constraints are met regardless of the underlying allocator implementation.

div.p: jemalloc organizes memory into size classes and slabs to minimize internal fragmentation and improve cache locality. Each size class has dedicated slabs—contiguous memory regions filled with same-sized allocations. This relates directly to our aligned_alloc() usage for SoA particle arrays.

ol.li: Reduced Fragmentation: Allocations rounded up to nearest size class.
Fast Allocation: O(1) from free list per size class bin.
Cache Alignment: Size classes ≥ 64 bytes are aligned to cache boundaries.
Predictable Layout: Same-sized objects grouped in contiguous slabs.

ul.li: For 64-byte size class: Each slot is exactly 1 cache line (64 bytes).
Slot boundaries align with L1/L2 cache line boundaries.
False sharing is minimized because allocations are isolated in discrete slots.

div.p: A slab is a contiguous memory region (typically 4KB-256KB) divided into fixed-size slots for a specific size class. Each allocation (malloc/aligned_alloc) takes one slot.
Thread 1 Obj | Thread 2 Obj ┌─────────────────────────┐ │ [64-byte Cache Line] │ └─────────────────────────┘ Thrashing on write!
